questions.text: A unit vector is obtained by dividing the given vector by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A unit vector is obtained by dividing the given vector by its magnitude or length. This process is done to normalize the vector, resulting in a vector that has a magnitude of 1 while retaining the direction of the original vector.

Dividing a vector by its angle doesn't yield a meaningful mathematical operation. Instead, to obtain a unit vector, you divide the vector by its magnitude (or length), not by its angle. The angle between vectors is used in various calculations, such as determining components or projections, but division of a vector by an angle isn't a standard mathematical operation.

Dividing one vector by another vector, isn't directly defined in standard vector algebra. There is a concept known as the "vector quotient," but it's not a typical arithmetic operation like addition or multiplication. It's often used in advanced mathematics or physics contexts, where the division of vectors might be represented in terms of the cross product and the inverse of a vector. However, straightforward division between two vectors in the way one might divide numbers is not a standard operation in vector mathematics.

questions.text: There are _ methods of adding two or more vectors.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: There are two primary methods of adding two or more vectors: graphical methods and analytical methods.1. Graphical Methods: These involve using geometric techniques like the head-to-tail method or the parallelogram method to add vectors. Graphical methods represent vectors as arrows in a diagram and add them by arranging these arrows in a specific geometric way to determine the resultant vector.2. Analytical Methods:Analytical methods involve using mathematical operations on the components of vectors. For instance, to add vectors in two dimensions, you add their respective x and y components separately. In three dimensions, you'd also consider the z components. This method relies on mathematical calculations rather than graphical representations. This method is commonly called vector addition by rectangular components.
There are two methods: haed to tail rule and addition of vectors by rectangular components.

Energy (E) is a scalar quantity that represents the ability to do work. The dot product is not directly associated with energy, as energy is not a vector quantity.

Work (W) is the product of the force applied to an object and the distance over which the force is applied, in the direction of the force. Mathematically, work is calculated using the dot product of force and displacement: W=F⋅d The dot product accounts for the directional component of the force that contributes to the work done.

Momentum (p) is a vector quantity representing the motion of an object. It is the product of mass and velocity. The dot product is not directly associated with momentum, as momentum involves the product of two vectors (mass and velocity).

"Factoring" in this case is just applying the distributive property in reverse, and since the distributive property holds for matrix multiplication, this is indeed possible.

questions.explanation: The Explanation: Solution The process of splitting a vector into its components is called resolution of the vector. The components, when added vectorially, will yield the same vector. Generally, we resolve a vector into three components viz. component along the x-axiscalled x-component component along the y-axis called y-component component along the z-axis called z-component
